LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SYLVIA FORSMANN MOTION TO AMEND SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT TO INCLUDE PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST DEFENDANT HOSPICE BY THE BAY. HEARING HELD. MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION AND DENIED TO ALL THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT CITES CCP SEC. 525.13, APPLIES TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION, EXCEPT ELDER ABUSE. COURT CITES GOODSTEIN VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 42 CAL.APP. 4TH 1635, COUNSEL'S ARGUMENT INSUFFICIENT AS TO 1ST FACTOR, AND INSUFFICIENT AS TO 2ND FACTOR, REASONABLE EFFECTS. COURT ALSO CITES CENTRAL PATHOLOGY SERVICE MEDICAL CLINIC, INC. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 3 CAL. 4TH 181, 182. THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOULD BE RE-DRAFTED TO CONFORM WITH THE COURT'S RULING. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HOSPICE BY THE BAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE EXISTENCE OF A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP. THE PRIMARY CONSIDERATION IS WHETHER THE SPECIAL EMPLOYER HAS THE RIGHT TO CONTROL AND DIRECT THE ACTIVITIES OF THE ALLEGED EMPLOYEE OR THE MANNER AND METHOD IN WHICH THE WORK IS PERFORMED. CASO VS. NIMROD PRODUCTIONS, INC. (2008) 163 CAL.APP. 4TH 881, 888-889. BILYCH TESTIFIED SHE HAD TO FOLLOW HBB'S REGISTERED NURSE'S DIRECTIONS AND WAS ACTING UNDER THE REGISTERED NURSE'S SUPERVISION. (BILYCK DEPO PGS. 60 AND 63.) A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED REGARDING CLOPTON'S KNOWLEDGE OF BILYCK'S ACTIVITIES. (CLOPTON DEPO 102-103; 108-110). (THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S SUR REPLY) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

Off Calendar Oct-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HOSPICE BY THE BAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. (Matter heard at 10:30 am.) Appearances: Christopher Dolan, The Dolan Law Firm (415) 421-2800 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Sheila F. Gonzalez, Esq. of William Schmidt & Assoc. (916) 447-2451 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication denied. Plaintiffs raise a triable issue of fact regarding the existence of a special relationship. The primary consideration is whether the special employer has the right to control and direct the activities of the alleged employee or the manner and method in which the work is performed. Caso vs. Nimrod Productions, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App. 4th 881, 888-889. Bilych testified she had to follow HBB's registered nurse's directions and was acting under the registered nurse's supervision. (Bilyck depo pgs. 60 and 63.) A triable issue of fact is raised regarding Clopton's knowledge of Bilyck's activities. (Clopton depo 102-103; 108-110). (The Court did not consider Plaintiff's sur reply) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
